note: Universal barrier type comparison. Containment levels reference EN 1317 and MASH systems.

data.typical_application: Embankments, fixed objects, standard rural roads. Most common barrier type worldwide.
data.maintenance_after_impact: Replace damaged sections (posts, rail). Moderate cost.

data.typical_application: Higher containment needs, bridge approach transitions, heavy vehicle routes.
data.maintenance_after_impact: Replace damaged sections. Higher cost than W-beam.

data.typical_application: Wide medians, roadside with adequate space for deflection. Low visual impact.
data.maintenance_after_impact: Re-tension cables, replace damaged posts. Relatively low cost per repair.

data.typical_application: Narrow medians, bridges, high-speed roads with no deflection space. Permanent installation.
data.maintenance_after_impact: Minimal for low-speed impacts. May need replacement for high-speed impacts.

data.typical_application: Urban areas with limited space. Aesthetic applications.
data.maintenance_after_impact: Replace damaged sections. Moderate to high cost.

data.typical_application: Upstream end of any barrier on roads with speed > 60 km/h. Absorbs head-on impact energy.
data.maintenance_after_impact: Replace after impact. Moderate to high cost per unit.

data.typical_application: Gore areas, bridge piers, tunnel portals. Freestanding installation protecting a fixed point.
data.maintenance_after_impact: Replace deformed elements. Some types are redirective (reusable after minor impact).
